2603.23183.title: Reasoning over Semantic IDs Enhances Generative Recommendation
2603.23183.paper_summary:
Recent advances in generative recommendation have leveraged pretrained LLMs by formulating sequential recommendation as autoregressive generation over a unified token space comprising language tokens and itemic identifiers, where each item is represented by a compact sequence of discrete tokens, namely Semantic IDs (SIDs). This SID-based formulation enables efficient decoding over large-scale item corpora and provides a natural interface for LLM-based recommenders to leverage rich world knowledge. Meanwhile, breakthroughs in LLM reasoning motivate reasoning-enhanced recommendation, yet effective reasoning over SIDs remains underexplored and challenging. Itemic tokens are not natively meaningful to LLMs; moreover, recommendation-oriented SID reasoning is hard to evaluate, making high-quality supervision scarce.
  To address these challenges, we propose SIDReasoner, a two-stage framework that elicits reasoning over SIDs by strengthening SID--language alignment to unlock transferable LLM reasoning, rather than relying on large amounts of recommendation-specific reasoning traces. Concretely, SIDReasoner first enhances SID-language alignment via multi-task training on an enriched SID-centered corpus synthesized by a stronger teacher model, grounding itemic tokens in diverse semantic and behavioral contexts. Building on this enhanced alignment, SIDReasoner further improves recommendation reasoning through outcome-driven reinforced optimization, which guides the model toward effective reasoning trajectories without requiring explicit reasoning annotations. Extensive experiments on three real-world datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our reasoning-augmented SID-based generative recommendation. Beyond accuracy, the results highlight the broader potential of large reasoning models for generative recommendation, including improved interpretability and cross-domain generalization.
2603.23183.paper_summary_zh: 最近在生成推薦方面的進展利用了預訓練的 LLM，通過將序列推薦表述為在統一的標記空間中進行自回歸生成，該空間包含語言標記和項目識別符，其中每個項目由一組緊湊的離散標記序列表示，即語義 ID (SIDs)。這種基於 SID 的表述使得在大規模項目語料庫上進行高效解碼成為可能，並為基於 LLM 的推薦系統提供了一個自然的接口，以利用豐富的世界知識。同時，LLM 推理的突破激勵了增強推理的推薦，但對 SIDs 的有效推理仍然未被充分探索且充滿挑戰。項目標記對 LLM 來說並不是本質上有意義的；此外，面向推薦的 SID 推理難以評估，這使得高質量的監督變得稀缺。<lb/>為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了 SIDReasoner，一個兩階段框架，通過加強 SID 與語言的對齊來引發對 SIDs 的推理，以解鎖可轉移的 LLM 推理，而不是依賴大量的推薦特定推理痕跡。具體而言，SIDReasoner 首先通過在由更強的教師模型合成的豐富 SID 中心語料庫上進行多任務訓練來增強 SID 與語言的對齊，將項目標記根植於多樣的語義和行為上下文中。在這種增強的對齊基礎上，SIDReasoner 進一步通過基於結果的強化優化來改善推薦推理，這引導模型朝向有效的推理軌跡，而不需要明確的推理註釋。在三個真實世界數據集上進行的大量實驗證明了我們的推理增強 SID 基於生成推薦的有效性。除了準確性，結果還突顯了大型推理模型在生成推薦中的更廣泛潛力，包括改善可解釋性和跨領域泛化。
2603.23183.author: Yingzhi He et.al.
2603.23183.authors: Yingzhi He, Yan Sun, Junfei Tan, Yuxin Chen, Xiaoyu Kong, Chunxu Shen, Xiang Wang, An Zhang, Tat-Seng Chua
